items.tip: Indicates whether the account is active.
The name associated with each account to be displayed on the LCD. (e.g., MyCompany)
The URL or IP address, and port of the SIP server. This is provided by your VoIP service provider (e.g., sip.mycompany.com, or IP address)
The URL or IP address, and port of the SIP server. This will be used when the primary SIP server fails.
IP address or Domain name of the Primary Outbound Proxy, Media Gateway, or Session Border Controller.
Defines secondary outbound proxy that will be used when the primary proxy cannot be connected.
User account information, provided by your VoIP service provider.
SIP service subscriber's Authenticate ID used for authentication. It can be identical to or different from the SIP User ID.
The account password required for the phone to authenticate with the SIP server before the account can be registered.
The SIP server subscriber's name (optional) that will be used for Caller ID display (e.g., John Doe).
Allows users to access voice messages by pressing the MESSAGE button on the phone. This value is usually the VM portal access number.
SIP service subscriber's Authenticate ID used for authentication. It can be identical to or different from the SIP User ID.
This parameter controls how the Search Appliance looks up IP addresses for hostnames. If "Use Configured IP" is selected, please fill in Primary IP, Backup IP 1 and Backup IP 2.

Sets the maximum number of retries for the device to send requests to the server. In DNS SRV configuration, if the destination address does not respond, all request messages are resent to the same address according to the configured retry times. Valid range: 1-10.

Configures the preferred method for DNS SRV failover. If "Default" is selected, the primary SIP server or Outbound Proxy will always be attempted first for all REGISTER and INVITE requests. If "Use current server until DNS TTL" is selected, the device will send all SIP messages to the current failover SIP server or Outbound Proxy until DNS times out. If "Use current server until no response" is selected, the device will send all SIP messages to the current failover SIP server or Outbound Proxy until there is no response. If "Use current server until failback timer expires" is selected, the device will send all SIP messages to the current failover SIP server or Outbound Proxy until the failback timer expires.
Specifies the duration (in minutes) since failover to the current SIP server or Outbound Proxy before making failback attempts to the primary SIP server or Outbound Proxy.
Configures whether to send REGISTER requests to the failover SIP server or Outbound Proxy before sending INVITE requests in the event of a DNS SRV failover.

Configures the Primary IP address. When "DNS Mode" is selected as "Use Configured IP Mode", and the SIP server and outbound proxy are configured as domain, no DNS requests will be sent. This IP address is configured as the result of domain name resolution, which is the destination of subsequent SIP messages.

Configures the backup IP 1 address. When "DNS Mode" is selected as "Use Configured IP Mode", and the SIP server and outbound proxy are configured as domain, no DNS requests will be sent. This IP address is configured as the result of domain name resolution, which is the destination of subsequent SIP messages.

Configures the backup IP 2 address. When "DNS Mode" is selected as "Use Configured IP Mode", and the SIP server and outbound proxy are configured as domain, no DNS requests will be sent. This IP address is configured as the result of domain name resolution, which is the destination of subsequent SIP messages.

Configures whether NAT traversal mechanism is activated. Please refer to user manual for more details.
  Set whether to activate NAT traversal mechanism. When set to "STUN" and specify the STUN server address, the detection will run based on the STUN server; When set to 'Keep-Alive', the phone will periodically send UDP packets to the SIP server to keep the NAT access port open; If the user uses OpenVPN®,  Please set it to 'OpenVPN'; If the user uses WireGuard®, Please set it to 'WireGuard'.
A SIP Extension to notify the SIP server that the phone is behind a NAT/Firewall.
Configures whether a SBC server is used. Note: If enabled, make sure an outbound proxy is set up.

If the phone has an assigned PSTN telephone number, this field should be set to "user=phone". A "user=phone" parameter will be attached to the Request-URI and "To" header in the SIP request to indicate the E.164 number. If set to "Enable", "tel:" will be used instead of "sip:" in the SIP request.
Selects whether the phone will send SIP Register messages to the proxy/server.

If set to "No", the phone will not unregister the SIP user's registration information before new registration. If set to "All", the SIP Contact header will use "*" to clear all SIP user's registration information. If set to "Instance", the phone only needs to clear the current SIP user's info.
Specifies the frequency (in minutes) in which the phone refreshes its registration with the specified registrar. The maximum value is 64800 (about 45 days).
Specifies the frequency (in minutes) in which the phone refreshes its subscription with the specified registrar. The maximum value is 64800 (about 45 days).
Specifies the time frequency (in seconds) that the phone sends re-registration request before the Register Expiration.
Configures whether to enable SIP OPTIONS to track account registration status. If enabled, the phone will send periodic OPTIONS messages to server to track the connection status with the server.
Configures the time interval the phone sends OPTIONS message to the server. If set to 30 seconds, it means the phone will send an OPTIONS message to the server every 30 seconds.

Configures the maximum number of times the phone will retry to send OPTIONS message consistently to server without receiving a response. If set to "3", the phone will send OPTIONS message 4 times. If no response from the server, the phone will re-register.
Configures whether to enable TCP Keep-alive for the TCP connection between the terminal and the SIP server.
Configures the local SIP port used to listen and transmit.
Specifies the interval to retry registration if the process failed. Valid range is 1 to 3600.
SIP T1 Timeout. Default value is 0.5 seconds.
SIP T2 Interval. Default value is 4 seconds.
Selects the network protocol used for the SIP transport.

Configures whether or not to listen to multiple SIP protocols. If set to "Dual", phone will listen to TCP when UDP is selected. If set to "Dual (Secured)", phone will listen to TLS/TCP when UDP is selected. If "TCP" or "TLS/TCP" is selected, UDP will be listened too. If set to "Dual (BLF Enforced)", phone will try to enforce BLF subscriptions to use TCP protocol by adding 'transport=tcp' to the Contact header.
Specifies if "sip:" or "sips:" will be used when TLS/TCP is selected for SIP Transport.
Configures whether the actual ephemeral port in contact with TCP/TLS will be used when TLS/TCP is selected for SIP Transport.
Configures whether to put the Outbound Proxy in the Route header, or if SIP messages should always be sent to Outbound Proxy.
Configures whether SIP Instance ID is supported or not.
When set to "Yes", a SUBSCRIBE for Message Waiting Indication will be sent periodically.
When set to "Yes", a SUBSCRIBE for Registration will be sent out periodically.
When enabled, the 100rel tag is appended to the value of the Supported header of the initial signaling messages.

When set to "Auto", the phone will update the callee ID in the order of P-Asserted Identity Header, Remote-Party-ID Header and To Header in 180 Ringing. When set to "Disabled", callee ID will be displayed as "Unavailable". When set to "To Header", caller ID will not be updated and displayed as To Header.

When set to "Auto", the phone will look for the Caller ID in the order of P-Asserted Identity Header, Remote-Party-ID Header and From Header in the incoming SIP INVITE. When set to "Disabled", all incoming calls are displayed with "Unavailable".
If enabled, the phone will add Authorization header in initial REGISTER request.
Configures to allow SIP notification message to perform factory reset.
Configures to play default ringtone by ignoring Alert-Info header.

Configures whether the "Privacy Header" is present in the SIP INVITE message. If set to "Default", the phone will add "Privacy Header" when special feature is not "Huawei IMS". If set to "NO", the phone will not add "Privacy Header". If set to "Yes", the phone will always add "Privacy Header".

Configures whether the "P-Preferred-Identity Header" is present in the SIP INVITE message. If set to "Default", the phone will add "P-Preferred-Identity header" when special feature is not "Huawei IMS". If set to "NO", the phone will not add "P-Preferred-Identity header". If set to "Yes", the phone will always add "P-Preferred-Identity header".
Configures to use X-Grandstream-PBX header in SIP request.
Configures to use P-Access-Network-Info header in SIP request.
Configures to use P-Emergency-Info header in SIP request.
Configure whether the "P-Asserted-Identity Header" is present in the SIP INVITE message.

If set to "Register Only", all outgoing SIP message will include the MAC header. If set to "Yes to all SIP", all outgoing SIP messages will include the MAC header. If set to "No", the phone's MAC header will not be included in any outgoing SIP messages.

If set to "Yes except REGISTER", all outgoing SIP messages will include the phone's MAC address in the User-Agent header, except for REGISTER and UNREGISTER. If set to "Yes to All SIP", all outgoing SIP messages will include the phone's MAC address in the User-Agent header. If set to "No", the phone's MAC address will not be included in the User-Agent header in any outgoing SIP messages.
Enables Presence feature on the phone.
Omit charset=UTF-8 in MESSAGE content-type
When enabled, DND and Call Forward features will be synchronized with the SIP server.
Specifies the server type for special requirements.

When select "Manual", the collector address will be manually configured and transmitted using the SIP Transport protocol. When select "Auto", if the collector address is manually filled in, use this address. If not filled in, use the SIP registered address.
Configures the host name of the central report collector that accepts voice quality reports contained in SIP PUBLISH messages.
Configures the IP address of the central report collector that accepts voice quality reports contained in SIP PUBLISH messages.
Configures the port of the central report collector that accepts voice quality reports contained in SIP PUBLISH messages.

Configures whether to enable session timer function. It enables SIP sessions to be periodically "refreshed" via a SIP request (UPDATE, or re-INVITE). If there is no refresh via an UPDATE or re-INVITE message, the session will be terminated once the session interval expires. If set to "Yes", the phone will use the related parameters when sending session timer according to "Session Expiration". If set to "No", session timer will be disabled.
Session Expiration is the time (in seconds) where the session is considered timed out, provided no successful session refresh transaction occurs beforehand.
The minimum session expiration (in seconds).
If set to "Yes" and the remote party supports SIP Session Timer, the phone will request the use of a Session Timer when it makes outbound calls.
If set to "Yes" and both parties support SIP Session Timer, the phone will request the use of a Session Timer when it receives inbound calls.
If set to "Yes", the phone will use the Session Timer even if the remote party does not support this feature. Otherwise, Session Timer is enabled only when the remote party supports it.
As a caller, select UAC to use the phone as the refresher, or select UAS to use the callee or proxy server as the refresher. When set to "Omit", the refresh object is not specified.
As a callee, select UAC to use caller or proxy server as the refresher, or select UAS to use the phone as the refresher.
Select "Yes" to force using the INVITE method to refresh the session timer.
Configures whether the domain certificates will be checked when TLS/TCP is used for SIP Transport.

Configure the list of trusted domain names, which supports filling in the
SAN list used only for domain name verification in TLS to obtain certificates.
If it matches any item in the trusted domain name list, the certificate is
trusted. By default, the remote proxy domain name and SIP server domain name
are trusted. This field allows numbers/letters/-/./*. It supports wildcard
domain names, such as “*.mycompany.com“, and trusts any domains ending with
“.mycompany.com“.
Validates certificate chain when TLS/TCP is configured
Specifies if the phone will check the incoming SIP messages Caller ID and CSeq headers. If the message does not include the headers, it will be rejected.
If set to "Yes", SIP User ID will be checked in the Request URI of the incoming INVITE. If it doesn't match the phone's SIP User ID, the call will be rejected.
When set to "Yes", the SIP address of the Request URL in the incoming SIP message will be checked. If it doesn't match the SIP server address of the account, the call will be rejected.
If set to "Yes", the phone will challenge the incoming INVITE for authentication with SIP 401 Unauthorized response.

When set to "Yes", the device will use the first matching vocoder in the received 200OK SDP as the codec.

Configures the phone to use specific codec sequence to negotiate as callee. When set to "Caller", the phone negotiates by SDP codec sequence from received SIP INVITE; When set to "Callee", the phone negotiates by audio codec sequence on the phone. The default setting is "Callee".
if enabled, the phone always responses 1 m line in SDP regardless multiple m lines are offered.
Enables and selects SRTP mode.
The cipher method/key length to use if SRTP is enabled.
Configures whether to enable Crypto Life Time.
Configures whether Symmetric RTP is used or not. Symmetric RTP means that the UA uses the same socket/port for sending and receiving the RTP stream. The default setting is "No".
If set to "Yes", when silence is detected, a small quantity of VAD packets (instead of audio packets) will be sent during the period of no talking. For codec G.723 and G.729 only.
Selects either Fixed or Adaptive based on network conditions.
Selects Low, Medium, or High based on network conditions.

Configures the number of voice frames transmitted per packet. It is recommended that the IS limit value of Ethernet packet is 1500 bytes or 120 kbps. When configuring this, it should be noted that the "ptime" value for the SDP will change with different configurations here. This value is related to the codec used in the codec table or negotiate the payload type during the actual call. For example, if set to 2 and the first code is G.729, G.711 or G.726, the "ptime" value in the SDP datagram of the INVITE request is 20 ms. If the "Voice Frame/TX" setting exceeds the maximum allowed value, the phone will use and save the maximum allowed value for the selected first codec. It is recommended to use the default setting provided, and incorrect setting may affect voice quality.
Selects encoding rate for G.723 codec.
Selects "ITU" or "IETF" for G.726-32 packing mode.
Selects iLBC packet frame size.
Specifies iLBC payload type. Valid range is 96 to 127. Cannot be the same as Opus or DTMF payload type.
Specifies Opus payload type. Valid range is 96 to 127. It cannot be the same as iLBC or DTMF Payload Type.
Configures the payload type for DTMF using RFC2833. Cannot be the same as iLBC or Opus payload type.
Specifies the mechanism to transmit DTMF digits.
Specifies the mechanism to transmit DTMF digits.
Specifies the mechanism to transmit DTMF digits.
Configures a prefix added to all numbers when making outbound calls.
Configures the dial plan rule. For syntax and examples, please refer to user manual for more details.
Bypass dial plan on selected items.
Configures to play reminder tone when the call is on hold.
Configures Call Log settings on the phone.
If set to "Yes", the "From" header in outgoing INVITE messages will be set to anonymous.

If set to "Yes", the "Refer-To" header uses the transferred target's Contact header information for attended transfer.
Configures whether the call is transferred to the other party if the conference initiator hangs up.
Configures the timeout (in seconds) when waiting for sipfrag response in blind transfer. Valid range is 30 to 300.

When set to "Caller", the phone negotiates by SDP codec sequence from received SIP INVITE; When set to "Callee", the phone negotiates by audio codec sequence on the phone. The default setting is "Callee".
translate.hs.4.user.automuteforautoanswer_desc: Enable mute when handset is auto answering
translate.account.{x}.audio.jitterbuffertype_desc: Selects either Fixed or Adaptive based on network conditions.
translate.network.port.eth.1.gateway.4_desc: Enter the Default Gateway when static IP is used.
translate.hs.9.user.disablecallwaitingtone_desc: Disables call waiting tone for the handset.
translate.account.{x}.audio.symmetricrtp_desc: Configures whether Symmetric RTP is used or not. Symmetric RTP means that the UA uses the same socket/port for sending and receiving the RTP stream. The default setting is "No".
translate.network.dns.1.ip.2_desc: Enter the DNS Server 1 when static IP is used.
translate.network.port.eth.1.type_desc:
Users could select "DHCP", "Static IP" or "PPPoE". 
• DHCP: Obtain IP address via a DHCP server in the LAN. All domain values for static IP/PPPoE are unavailable, even though the values have been saved in the flash.
• PPPoE: Configures PPPoE account/password. Obtain the IP address from the PPPoE server via dialing.
• Static IP: Manually configures IP Address, Subnet Mask, Default Router's IP Address, DNS Server 1 and DNS Server 2.
translate.phonebook.download.password_desc: The password for the HTTP/HTTPS server.
translate.account.{x}.ring.match.4.callerid_desc: Specifies matching rules with number, pattern or Alert Info text to ring the selected ringtone. Please refer to user manual for more details.
translate.ons.actionurl.closedforward_desc: Send the Action URL when the IP phone disables call forward function.
translate.account.{x}.codec.hidelabel_desc: Hide vocoder information on call screen.
translate.hs.2.admin.offhookautodialdelay_desc: Defines the timeout (in seconds) for off-hook auto dial. Valid range is 0-60. If set to 0, it will be dialed out immediately; If set to other values, it will be dialed out after the delay.
translate.hs.4.admin.allowHangupACallInHoldingStat: Allow Hang up A Call in Holding State
translate.account.{x}.call.refertousetargetcontact: If set to "Yes", the "Refer-To" header uses the transferred target's Contact header information for attended transfer.
translate.hs.10.intercom.playWarningToneForAutoAns: Play Warning Tone for Auto Answer Intercom
translate.provisioning.config.password_desc: The password for the config server.
translate.provisioning.auto.randomtime.enable_desc: Configures whether the phone will upgrade automatically at a random time within the configured time interval.
translate.ons.actionurl.terminatedcall_desc: Configures the Action URL to send when phone terminates a call.
translate.hs.3.user.enableansweroncradlepickup_des: Enables offhook of handset when picked up from cradle.
translate.account.{x}.sip.validate.domaincertifica: Configures whether the domain certificates will be checked when TLS/TCP is used for SIP Transport.
translate.account.{x}.call.rfc2543hold.enable_desc: If set to "Yes", c=0.0.0.0 will be used in INVITE SDP for hold.
translate.hs.2.user.enableansweroncradlepickup_des: Enables offhook of handset when picked up from cradle.
translate.network.web.port.https_desc: Configures the HTTPS port under the HTTPS web access mode.
translate.hs.7.user.disableRemoteDissconnectBusyTo: Disable Busy Tone on Remote Disconnect
translate.hs.8.user.disableremotedissconnectbusyto: Disable the busy tone heard in the handset when call is disconnected remotely.
translate.security.webaccess.user.enable_desc: Administrator can disable or enable user web access
translate.hs.7.user.customringtone_desc: Specifies a custom ringtone for this handset from the ringtones available on this base station.
translate.provisioning.handset.enable_desc: Enable handset config upgrade for handset related settings.
translate.account.{x}.codec.choice.3_desc: Multiple vocoder types are supported on the phone, the vocoders in the list are preferred. Users can configure vocoders in a preference list that is included with the same preference order in SDP message.
translate.hs.8.user.enablehanguponcradlereposition: Enables onhook of handset when repositioned on cradle.
translate.hs.4.user.customringtone_desc: Specifies a custom ringtone for this handset from the ringtones available on this base station.
translate.sip.returncode.dnd_desc: Configures the return code that phone will send when it has DND enabled.
translate.hs.8.user.disableRemoteDissconnectBusyTo: Disable Busy Tone on Remote Disconnect
translate.ldap.version_desc: Selects the protocol version for the phone to send the bind requests.
translate.account.{x}.codec.payloadtype.dtmf_desc: Configures the payload type for DTMF using RFC2833. Cannot be the same as iLBC or Opus payload type.
translate.broadsoftxsi.actionspath_desc: Configures the deployment path for BroadSoft Xsi Actions. If it is empty, the path "com.broadsoft.xsi-actions" will be used.
translate.network.lldp.txinterval_desc: Configures the interval the device sends LLDP-MED packets.
translate.ldap.username_desc: Configures the bind "Username" for querying LDAP servers. The field can be left blank if the LDAP server allows anonymous binds.
translate.hs.9.user.disableRemoteDissconnectBusyTo: Disable Busy Tone on Remote Disconnect
translate.provisioning.firmware.filepostfix_desc: If configured, only the firmware with the matching postfix will be downloaded and flashed into the phone.
translate.broadsoft.xsi.port.2_desc: Port of the Broadsoft XSI server
translate.hs.1.user.autodial_desc: Configures the digits to be dialed via the first account when the phone is off-hook.
translate.network.port.eth.1.address.3_desc: Enter the IP address when static IP is used.
translate.sip.override.dhcp.allowOption120: Allow DHCP Option 120 to Override SIP Server
translate.hs.5.user.enablemuteinidleasdnd_desc: Enable Mute Key as Global DND Key at Idle Screen.
translate.maintain.syslog.capture.continuous.timer: The file writing will automatically stop after the timeout period is reached.
translate.account.{x}.call.transferonconferencehan: Configures whether the call is transferred to the other party if the conference initiator hangs up.
translate.hs.5.call.nokeyentrytimeout_desc: Defines the timeout (in seconds) for no key entry. If no key is pressed after the timeout, the digits will be sent out.
translate.account.{x}.sip.subscriber.password_desc: The account password required for the phone to authenticate with the SIP server before the account can be registered.
translate.account.{x}.network.backupip.2_desc: Configures the backup IP 2 address. When "DNS Mode" is selected as "Use Configured IP Mode", and the SIP server and outbound proxy are configured as domain, no DNS requests will be sent. This IP address is configured as the result of domain name resolution, which is the destination of subsequent SIP messages.
translate.account.{x}.sip.keepalive.interval_desc: Configures the time interval the phone sends OPTIONS message to the server. If set to 30 seconds, it means the phone will send an OPTIONS message to the server every 30 seconds.
translate.hs.1.user.disableconference_desc: Disables the conference option on this handset.
translate.security.validate.servercertificate_desc: Configures whether to validate the server certificate when downloading the firmware/config file. If set to "Yes", the phone will download the firmware/config file only after the server is validated.
translate.account.{x}.sip.allowunsolicitedrefer_de: Configures whether to dial the number carried by Refer-to header after receiving out-of-dialog SIP REFER request actively. If set to "Disabled", the phone will send error warning and stop dialing. If set to "Enabled/Force Auth", the phone will dial the number after sending authentication. If the authentication fails, it will stop dialing. If set to "Enabled", the phone will dial all numbers carried by SIP REFER.
translate.ons.actionurl.setupcompleted_desc: Configures the Action URL to send when phone finishes setup process.
translate.ons.actionurl.unregistered_desc: Configures the Action URL to send when phone deregisters a SIP account.
translate.hs.9.user.autodial_desc: Configures the digits to be dialed via the first account when the phone is off-hook.
